name: French Open
description: The best spring tennis trip on the calendar — Paris, clay, and all-day grounds access if you do it right.

name: Wimbledon
description: Grand Slam grass court tournament in London.

name: Rotterdam Open
description: ATP 500 indoor hard court tournament in Rotterdam.

name: Libema Open
description: ATP grass court tournament in Den Bosch.

name: Davis Cup
description: International team tennis competition.

name: Ajax
description: Ajax season tickets and match tickets for the Eredivisie.

name: Feyenoord
description: Feyenoord season tickets and match tickets for the Eredivisie.

name: PSV
description: PSV season tickets and match tickets for the Eredivisie.

name: Netherlands National Team
description: Dutch national football team match tickets.

name: World Cup 2026
description: The biggest ticket in sport, with matches across the USA, Canada and Mexico.
notes: You need a FIFA account to enter the ticket ballot. Register at fifa.com/tickets before the sale opens on 2 April — accounts without prior registration may not be eligible.

name: European Championship
description: UEFA European Championship.

name: Club World Cup
description: FIFA Club World Cup.

name: Ryder Cup
description: Biennial golf competition between Europe and USA at Adare Manor, Ireland.
notes: Tickets for the Ryder Cup are sold via ballot only. Register your interest at rydercup.com — no sale date confirmed yet, but ballot registration is open now.

name: Solheim Cup
description: Biennial women's golf competition between Europe and USA.

name: The Open Championship
description: Golf's best major for atmosphere — links golf, weather, and a leaderboard that usually gets serious fast.

name: KLM Open
description: Dutch Open golf tournament.

name: Six Nations
description: Annual rugby union competition between England, France, Ireland, Italy, Scotland and Wales.

name: Hockey European Championship
description: European Hockey Championship.

name: Hockey World Cup
description: FIH Hockey World Cup.

name: Hoofdklasse Play-offs
description: The sharpest hockey of the Dutch season, with finals that usually feel quicker and louder than the regular calendar.

name: Museumplein Polo Amsterdam
description: Polo in the cultural heart of Amsterdam, surrounded by the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum and Concertgebouw. 9 matches over three days. General admission is free.
spotlight_description: Polo is one of the oldest team sports in the world — two teams of four riders compete on horseback to drive a small ball through the opposing goal using a long mallet. It is fast, physical and surprisingly easy to follow live. Museumplein Polo Amsterdam brings the sport to one of the most iconic squares in Europe, with the Rijksmuseum as the backdrop. General access is free, making it an easy day out.

name: FIBA 3x3 World Tour Amsterdam Masters
description: Fast, compact, and easy to watch live — the best 3x3 players in the world at the NDSM wharf.
spotlight_description: 3x3 is one of the easiest sports to enjoy live: short games, one court, constant action. The Amsterdam Masters brings the best players in the world to the NDSM wharf and works best as part of a bigger day out — music, food and the rest of Urban Sports Week around it.

name: Amstel Tigers Ice Hockey
description: Amsterdam's ice hockey team competing in the Dutch Eredivisie. Home games at Jaap Edenbaan.
spotlight_description: Ice hockey is one of the most intense live sports you can watch — fast skating, physical play and goals that happen in the blink of an eye. The Amstel Tigers are Amsterdam's professional team, playing in the Dutch Eredivisie at the Jaap Edenbaan. The rink atmosphere is unlike any other sport: close to the action, loud and social. Tickets are affordable and the games are usually sold out for big matchups.

name: World Sprint Speed Skating Championships
description: The world's best sprinters at Thialf in Heerenveen. 500m and 1000m for men and women.
spotlight_description: Sprint skating is the most explosive form of long-track speed skating. At World Championship level, men cover 500 metres in under 35 seconds — faster than most people can follow. Thialf in Heerenveen is the fastest ice in the world and almost every World Championship held there results in world records. The atmosphere is uniquely Dutch: a sold-out arena full of orange flags, hand-tracked times on the scoreboard and a crowd that celebrates every hundredth of a second.

name: Holland's Strongest Man
description: Annual competition to find the strongest man in the Netherlands. Events include truck pulls, log lifts and atlas stones.
spotlight_description: Strongman competitions are exactly what they sound like — a series of events designed to find the strongest human being. Athletes pull trucks, flip tyres, carry fridges and lift boulders, all against the clock. Holland's Strongest Man is the national qualifier for the World's Strongest Man and attracts some of the best athletes in the sport. It is one of the most crowd-friendly live events you can attend: the feats of strength are so extreme they are almost comical, and the athletes genuinely enjoy the crowd interaction.

name: Premier Padel Rotterdam
description: Premier Padel tournament at Rotterdam Ahoy. The world's best padel players, qualifications 27-28 September, main draw 29 September – 4 October.
notes: Day and evening sessions require separate tickets.
spotlight_description: Premier Padel is the highest competitive level in padel — comparable to the ATP Tour in tennis. Rotterdam Ahoy transforms into a padel arena with a centre court and multiple side courts. The world's best players compete here for world ranking points. For anyone who hasn't seen padel live: the speed and precision at this level is completely different from recreational play. Accessible prices and a great atmosphere in a compact venue.

name: Urban Sports Week Amsterdam
description: A genuinely fun Amsterdam sports weekend — 3x3, skate, BMX, parkour and a proper festival atmosphere at the NDSM wharf.
spotlight_description: Urban Sports Week is one of the easiest sports events in Amsterdam to say yes to. You get the FIBA 3x3 World Tour as the centrepiece, but also skate, BMX and parkour across the site, plus music and food. It feels more like a festival with serious sport in the middle of it than a formal event.

name: 3x3NL Tour Finals Amsterdam
description: The final of the national 3x3 basketball competition, held at Hoekerodeplein in Amsterdam.
spotlight_description: The 3x3NL Tour is the national competition for 3x3 basketball with stops across the Netherlands. The Tour Finals are held annually in Amsterdam at the Hoekerodeplein. It is street basketball at the highest Dutch level — outdoor, free to watch and close to the action. The atmosphere is urban and accessible, perfect as an introduction to the sport.

name: Jumping Amsterdam
description: International equestrian event at RAI Amsterdam. World Cup show jumping and dressage with the world's best riders.
spotlight_description: Jumping Amsterdam is one of the most important equestrian events in Europe and part of the FEI World Cup. At the RAI in Amsterdam, the world's best show jumpers and dressage riders compete for World Cup points. For anyone who has never seen this live: the combination of power, precision and the bond between rider and horse is extraordinary. The RAI venue makes it intimate despite the high level, and the atmosphere is formal yet accessible.

name: Tilburg Trappers Ice Hockey
description: The Tilburg Trappers compete in the Oberliga (German second tier) and are the most successful ice hockey club in the Netherlands. Home games in Tilburg with consistently sold-out playoffs.
spotlight_description: The Tilburg Trappers are by far the most successful ice hockey club in the Netherlands. They compete in the German-Austrian Oberliga — a level far above the Dutch league — and have won multiple consecutive championships. Home games in Tilburg sell out quickly, especially the playoffs. Ice hockey is fast, physical and follows its own rhythm that you only understand when you see it live. An evening at the Trappers is one of the best live sports experiences in the Netherlands.

name: Summer BeachLife Volleyball, Scheveningen
description: Weekly recurring beach volleyball tournament on Scheveningen beach throughout the summer, with competitive divisions and a beach festival atmosphere.
spotlight_description: Summer BeachLife Volleyball is a weekly recurring beach volleyball tournament on Scheveningen beach throughout the entire summer. With the North Sea as backdrop and multiple courts side by side, it is one of the most enjoyable ways to watch live beach volleyball. The atmosphere is relaxed, entry is free or low-cost, and the level ranges from recreational to seriously competitive. Perfect for combining a day at the coast with sport.
